methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terranova is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $7M to date. The valuation exercise draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms and one public comparable, though narrow peer filtering at identical stage and valuation tier yielded insufficient datapoints for median-based triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($7M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $18M. This method captures the structural progression expected as Terranova advances to Series A, though it carries the limitation inherent to all stage-based models: it does not account for company-specific operating metrics, product maturity, or competitive positioning within the robotics sector.

The peer-median method could not be applied. While 11 robotics and manufacturing companies exist in the broader comp set—including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics—filtering for same-stage and adjacent-stage peers at similar valuation tiers produced fewer than the three datapoints required for robust median estimation. This analysis therefore supports a valuation range of $15M to $20M, centered at $18M, pending access to either comparable seed-stage transaction data or forward-looking unit economics that would enable revenue-multiple or DCF validation.
